_readme: Real per-state facts used to seed state-specific opening sentences across engines. When a specific number is not verifiable, a qualitative descriptor is used. SHIP national locator: 1-877-839-2675. Eldercare Locator: 1-800-677-1116.

alabama.shipName: Alabama State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
alabama.taxesSsNote: Alabama does not tax Social Security retirement benefits.
alabama.aduUnit: Alabama does not have an SSP; SSI recipients receive the federal benefit only.

alaska.taxesSsNote: Alaska has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed at the state level.
alaska.ssiSupplement: Alaska Adult Public Assistance (APA) supplements SSI for aged, blind, and disabled residents.

arizona.medicaidProgram: Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System (AHCCCS)
arizona.shipName: Arizona SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
arizona.taxesSsNote: Arizona does not tax Social Security retirement benefits.
arizona.insuranceCommissioner: Arizona Department of Insurance and Financial Institutions

arkansas.shipName: Arkansas Senior Health Insurance Information Program (SHIIP)
arkansas.taxesSsNote: Arkansas does not tax Social Security benefits.

california.shipName: Health Insurance Counseling and Advocacy Program (HICAP)
california.taxesSsNote: California does not tax Social Security benefits.
california.ssiSupplement: California pays a State Supplementary Payment (SSP) on top of federal SSI.

colorado.vaDept: Colorado Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
colorado.taxesSsNote: Colorado taxes Social Security benefits, but residents 65+ can deduct a significant portion of retirement income under state rules.
colorado.ssiSupplement: Colorado provides a state supplement to SSI through Old Age Pension (OAP).

connecticut.taxesSsNote: Connecticut partially taxes Social Security benefits, with exemptions based on income thresholds.
connecticut.ssiSupplement: Connecticut provides a State Supplement to federal SSI.

delaware.medicaidProgram: Delaware Medicaid and Delaware Healthy Children Program
delaware.taxesSsNote: Delaware does not tax Social Security benefits.

district-of-columbia.medicaidProgram: DC Medicaid (Department of Health Care Finance)
district-of-columbia.taxesSsNote: The District of Columbia does not tax Social Security benefits.
district-of-columbia.insuranceCommissioner: DC Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking

florida.shipName: SHINE (Serving Health Insurance Needs of Elders)
florida.taxesSsNote: Florida has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.
florida.ssiSupplement: Florida provides Optional State Supplementation (OSS) for certain assisted living residents.

georgia.taxesSsNote: Georgia does not tax Social Security benefits and offers generous retirement income exclusions for seniors 62+.
georgia.insuranceCommissioner: Georgia Office of Commissioner of Insurance

hawaii.taxesSsNote: Hawaii does not tax Social Security benefits.
hawaii.ssiSupplement: Hawaii pays a State Supplementary Payment to SSI recipients in domiciliary care.

idaho.taxesSsNote: Idaho does not tax Social Security benefits.
idaho.ssiSupplement: Idaho provides a state supplement (AABD) to certain SSI recipients.

illinois.taxesSsNote: Illinois does not tax Social Security benefits or most retirement income.
illinois.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories

indiana.taxesSsNote: Indiana does not tax Social Security benefits.
indiana.ssiSupplement: Indiana provides Room and Board Assistance in Residential Settings (RBARS).

iowa.shipName: Iowa SHIIP (Senior Health Insurance Information Program)
iowa.taxesSsNote: Iowa does not tax Social Security benefits.
iowa.ssiSupplement: Iowa provides a state supplement for residents in residential care facilities.

kansas.shipName: Senior Health Insurance Counseling for Kansas (SHICK)
kansas.taxesSsNote: Kansas taxes Social Security for higher-income filers, but exempts benefits for residents with federal AGI below the state threshold.

kentucky.taxesSsNote: Kentucky does not tax Social Security benefits.

louisiana.countyTermNote: Louisiana is divided into 64 parishes rather than counties.
louisiana.taxesSsNote: Louisiana does not tax Social Security benefits.

maine.taxesSsNote: Maine does not tax Social Security benefits.
maine.ssiSupplement: Maine provides Supplemental Security Income State Program (SSI-SSP).

maryland.taxesSsNote: Maryland does not tax Social Security benefits.
maryland.ssiSupplement: Maryland does not provide a general state SSI supplement.

massachusetts.shipName: SHINE (Serving the Health Insurance Needs of Everyone)
massachusetts.taxesSsNote: Massachusetts does not tax Social Security benefits.
massachusetts.ssiSupplement: Massachusetts pays a state supplement to federal SSI (EAEDC/SSP).

michigan.taxesSsNote: Michigan does not tax Social Security benefits.
michigan.ssiSupplement: Michigan State Supplementation (SSP) for certain SSI recipients in adult foster care.
michigan.insuranceCommissioner: Michigan Department of Insurance and Financial Services

minnesota.taxesSsNote: Minnesota partially taxes Social Security benefits but offers a subtraction for qualifying filers; recent legislation has expanded the exemption.

mississippi.taxesSsNote: Mississippi does not tax Social Security benefits.

missouri.shipName: Missouri CLAIM (Community Leaders Assisting the Insured of Missouri)
missouri.taxesSsNote: Missouri eliminated state tax on Social Security benefits beginning in 2024 for all filers.
missouri.ssiSupplement: Missouri Supplemental Nursing Care and Blind Pension are available.
missouri.insuranceCommissioner: Missouri Department of Commerce and Insurance

montana.taxesSsNote: Montana partially taxes Social Security benefits, following federal treatment with a state-specific deduction.
montana.insuranceCommissioner: Montana Commissioner of Securities and Insurance

nebraska.taxesSsNote: Nebraska phased out state tax on Social Security benefits as of 2024.
nebraska.ssiSupplement: Nebraska provides a state supplement for certain SSI recipients.

nevada.medicaidProgram: Nevada Medicaid and Nevada Check Up
nevada.taxesSsNote: Nevada has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.

new-hampshire.vaDept: New Hampshire Office of Veterans Services
new-hampshire.taxesSsNote: New Hampshire does not tax Social Security benefits (state tax applies only to interest and dividends historically).
new-hampshire.ssiSupplement: New Hampshire provides a state supplement (OAA) for eligible elderly and disabled residents.

new-jersey.shipName: NJ State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
new-jersey.vaDept: New Jersey Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
new-jersey.taxesSsNote: New Jersey does not tax Social Security benefits.
new-jersey.ssiSupplement: New Jersey provides a State Supplement to federal SSI.
new-jersey.insuranceCommissioner: New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance

new-mexico.vaDept: New Mexico Department of Veterans' Services
new-mexico.taxesSsNote: New Mexico taxes Social Security for higher-income filers; benefits are exempt below state income thresholds updated in 2022.
new-mexico.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories
new-mexico.insuranceCommissioner: New Mexico Office of Superintendent of Insurance

new-york.shipName: HIICAP (Health Insurance Information, Counseling and Assistance Program)
new-york.vaDept: New York State Department of Veterans' Services
new-york.taxesSsNote: New York does not tax Social Security benefits.
new-york.ssiSupplement: New York pays the State Supplement Program (SSP) on top of federal SSI.
new-york.insuranceCommissioner: New York State Department of Financial Services

north-carolina.vaDept: North Carolina Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
north-carolina.taxesSsNote: North Carolina does not tax Social Security benefits.
north-carolina.ssiSupplement: North Carolina State/County Special Assistance for residents in adult care homes.

north-dakota.vaDept: North Dakota Department of Veterans Affairs
north-dakota.taxesSsNote: North Dakota does not tax Social Security benefits as of recent legislation.

ohio.shipName: Ohio Senior Health Insurance Information Program (OSHIIP)
ohio.taxesSsNote: Ohio does not tax Social Security benefits.
ohio.ssiSupplement: Ohio provides a State Supplement (Residential State Supplement) for certain SSI recipients.

oklahoma.taxesSsNote: Oklahoma does not tax Social Security benefits.
oklahoma.ssiSupplement: Oklahoma State Supplemental Payment for aged, blind, and disabled.

oregon.taxesSsNote: Oregon does not tax Social Security benefits.

pennsylvania.vaDept: Pennsylvania Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
pennsylvania.taxesSsNote: Pennsylvania does not tax Social Security benefits or most retirement income for residents 59.5+.

rhode-island.medicaidProgram: Rhode Island Medicaid (RIte Care, Rhody Health)
rhode-island.vaDept: Rhode Island Office of Veterans Services
rhode-island.taxesSsNote: Rhode Island partially taxes Social Security benefits, with an exemption for modest-income filers who are full retirement age.
rhode-island.ssiSupplement: Rhode Island pays a State Supplement Program on SSI.
rhode-island.insuranceCommissioner: Rhode Island Department of Business Regulation, Insurance Division

south-carolina.shipName: SC Insurance Counseling, Assistance, and Referrals for Elders (I-CARE)
south-carolina.vaDept: South Carolina Department of Veterans' Affairs
south-carolina.taxesSsNote: South Carolina does not tax Social Security benefits.
south-carolina.ssiSupplement: Optional State Supplement for residents in community residential care facilities.

south-dakota.vaDept: South Dakota Department of Veterans Affairs
south-dakota.taxesSsNote: South Dakota has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.

tennessee.taxesSsNote: Tennessee does not tax Social Security benefits (the state repealed the Hall income tax in 2021).
tennessee.insuranceCommissioner: Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance

texas.taxesSsNote: Texas has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.
texas.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories

utah.vaDept: Utah Department of Veterans and Military Affairs
utah.taxesSsNote: Utah taxes Social Security benefits but offers a non-refundable credit that eliminates tax for qualifying lower- and middle-income filers.

vermont.taxesSsNote: Vermont partially taxes Social Security benefits but exempts benefits for filers below state AGI thresholds.
vermont.ssiSupplement: Vermont provides a state supplement (Aid to the Aged, Blind, and Disabled).

virginia.shipName: Virginia Insurance Counseling and Assistance Program (VICAP)
virginia.countyTermNote: Virginia is unusual in having 38 independent cities that are not part of any county.
virginia.taxesSsNote: Virginia does not tax Social Security benefits.
virginia.ssiSupplement: Auxiliary Grants for residents in assisted living or adult foster care.

washington.vaDept: Washington State Department of Veterans Affairs
washington.taxesSsNote: Washington has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.
washington.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories
washington.insuranceCommissioner: Washington State Office of the Insurance Commissioner

west-virginia.vaDept: West Virginia Department of Veterans Assistance
west-virginia.taxesSsNote: West Virginia phased out state tax on Social Security benefits as of tax year 2022.
west-virginia.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories
west-virginia.insuranceCommissioner: West Virginia Offices of the Insurance Commissioner

wisconsin.taxesSsNote: Wisconsin does not tax Social Security benefits.
wisconsin.ssiSupplement: Wisconsin State Supplemental Payment (SSI-E) for elderly and disabled.
wisconsin.insuranceCommissioner: Wisconsin Office of the Commissioner of Insurance

wyoming.shipName: Wyoming State Health Insurance Information Program (WSHIIP)
wyoming.taxesSsNote: Wyoming has no state income tax, so Social Security benefits are not taxed.
wyoming.ssiSupplement: no state supplement for most categories
